info.title: API Reference
info.description:
## Status Codes and Errors

Striven REST API uses standard HTTP codes when returning API response.Error details are are provided in the response body whereever applicable.

Status Code Description
200 OK The API call is successful.
400 Bad Request The parameters supplied with the request are invalid. Response body will contain details about which parameter is incorrect.
401 Unauthorized OAuth access token is invalid.It might have been expired.
404 Not Found The requested resource is not found.
429 Too Many Requests Too many requests were sent to the API in a given amount of time.See the rate limits that correspond to your Striven plan for details.
500 Internal Server Error Unexpected error occured at the API server.These errors are logged in system and will be reviewed and fixed as they occur.

## Rate Limits

To ensure the reliability of the API, and to keep it available to all users, the Striven API enforces rate limits based on your Striven plan. We enforce both a per-minute rate limit, and a daily usage limit. Daily usage tracking resets at midnight UTC.

Requests that exceed either limit will receive a 429 Too Many Requests response, which contains the standard Retry-After header that indicates how many seconds a client should wait before retrying the request. Limits are allocated per tenant, so if a tenant has multiple clients consuming the API, an individual client could receive a 429 response without necessarily exceeding the rate themselves.

If you upgrade to the Enterprise plan, please allow 60 minutes for your rate limits to be increased.

Striven Plan Max Requests per Minute Max Requests per Day
Standard 100 5000
Enterprise 500 25,000

Example

This is a sample response a tenant on the Standard plan would receive when they exceed the per-minute Rate Limit.

Request

                GET https://api.striven.com/v1/shipping-methods HTTP/1.1
                Authorization: Bearer [your-access-token]

Response

                HTTP/1.1 429 Too Many Requests
                Content-Type: application/json
                Retry-After: 60
                {
                    "message": "You have reached the per-minute API usage limit for your Striven plan. The Standard plan allows 100 API calls per minute. Please wait 1 minute, or contact your administrator to upgrade to the Enterprise plan. If you upgrade to the Enterprise plan, please allow 60 minutes for this change to take effect."
                }
## Maintenance Schedule

Striven API might not be available during the following maintenance schedule.

Event When Description
Maintenance Window Every week Saturday from 2AM - 6AM Eastern Standard Time During this time Striven infrastructure goes under scheduled maintenance.
Release Based on the announcement made. Mostly, Sundays 7 AM Eastern Standard Time During this time a newer version of Striven is released.

post.summary: Uploads a document to the project's hub.
post.description:
This API expects a multipart/form-data encoded request containing the full contents of the file to be uploaded.
If you want to upload documents to a group by its name, then specify the group name in the header with this key: Content-Group-Name. 
If the group does not exist, then a new group with this name will be created. In this case you don't have to pass groupId as URI parameter.
If the content group Id or Name are not provided the the system will upload the document to the default content group.
Specify the Overwrite-Existing-Files header (true|false) to overwrite the existing file(s). Specify Visible-On-Portal header (true|false) to set visibility of uploaded documents on the Striven Customer/Vendor Portal.

**Required Permissions:** Projects - Read & Write

post.summary: Uploads a document to the project's hub.
post.description:
This API expects a multipart/form-data encoded request containing the full contents of the file to be uploaded.
If you want to upload documents to a group by its name, then specify the group name in the header with this key: Content-Group-Name. 
If the group does not exist, then a new group with this name will be created. In this case you don't have to pass groupId as URI parameter.
If the content group Id or Name are not provided the the system will upload the document to the default content group.
Specify the Overwrite-Existing-Files header (true|false) to overwrite the existing file(s). Specify Visible-On-Portal header (true|false) to set visibility of uploaded documents on the Striven Customer/Vendor Portal.

**Required Permissions:** Projects - Read & Write

If you want to upload documents to a group by its name, then specify the group name in the header with this key: Content-Group-Name. 
If the group does not exist, then a new group with this name will be created. In this case you don't have to pass groupId as URI parameter.

Type.description: Task Type accepts id or name. If both are provided id takes the precedence.

Priority.description: Task Priority accepts id or name. If both are provided id takes the precedence.

Customer.description: Customer to be associated with the task. Accepts id, number or name. If all of them are provided, id takes the first precedence, then the number and then the name.

Location.description: Customer Location to be associated with the task. Location is accepted only if the task type includes location. Accepts id or name. If both are provided id takes the precedence.

Budget.description: Budget is only accepted if the task type includes budget. Budget should be greater than or equal to zero.

TaskPercentageComplete.description: Task completed percentage is only accepted if the task type includes budget.

StartDateTime.description: Start date is only accepted if the task type includes start date. Accepts time part if the task type includes time for start date. Date and time will be accepted in ISO 8601 format.

DueDateTime.description: Time part for due date is accepted only if the task type includes time for due date. Date and time will be accepted in ISO 8601 format.

SalesOrder.description: Sales Order to be associated with the task. Accepts id, number or name. If all of them are provided, id takes the first precedence, then the number and then the name.

Project.description: Project to be associated with the task, accepts id or name. If both are provided id takes the precedence.

Milestone.description: Milestone to be associated with the task, accepts id or name. If both are provided id takes the precedence.

RequestedBy.description: Requested By is only accepted if the task type includes requested by.

UseSubContractor.description: Accepts only if the task type can be a subcontracted work.

PurchaseOrder.description: Purchase Order to be associated with the task. Accepts id, number or name. If all of them are provided, id takes the first precedence, then the number and then the name.

TaskPatch.description: You may only provide the properties that needs updating instead of whole object in the body. For example, in order to update Title, just supply Title in the request body.    /// You may supply null as the value of attribute to clear it from the task. Clearing is only allowed if the field is not required based on task type settings. Any parameter that are not include per task type configuration will be ignored.

Title.description: Task title. This parameter is required and cannot be cleared.

Priority.description: Task priority. This parameter is required and cannot be cleared.

Budget.description: Only accepted if the task type includes budget. Required when task type requires budget.

TaskPercentageComplete.description: Percentage complete is only applicable when budget is greater than 0.

StartDateTime.description:
Start date and time in ISO 8601 format. Accepted if task type includes Start Date. Time part is only accepted if task type includes start time. If no timezone information is provided, tenant's timezone will be assumed.
Required if task type requires Start Date.

DueDateTime.description:
Due date and time in ISO 8601 format. Time part is only accepted if task type includes due time. If no timezone information is provided, tenant's timezone will be assumed.
Required if task type requires Due Date.

Customer.description: The customer/vendor associated with the task. Required if task type requires Customer.

Location.description: Location of task for the customer selected. Only accepted if task type includes location, required if the task type requires location.

SalesOrder.description: Sales Order related to the task. Only accepted if task type includes sales order. Required if task type requires sales order. The provided sales order must be related to the customer linked to the task and also match other status configuration based on task type settings.

Project.description: Project related to the task. Only accepted if task type includes project.

Milestone.description: Milestone related to the task. Milestone must be related to the project linked to the task.

RequestedBy.description: Requested By either, cane be an employee or a contact that is related to customer linked on the task. Only accepted if task type includes Requested By, required if task type requires Requested By.

UseSubContractor.description: Accepts if task type can be a sub contracted work.

PurchaseOrder.description: Purchase Order related to subcontracted task. Only accepted and requried when UseSubcontractor flag is set to true

IsRequestedBy.description: Says whether the contact is set as Requested By to the task

Invoice.description:
If a sales order does not get billed automatically as per settings, then setting this field to true will generate an invoice for the remaining items that have not been invoiced related to this order. 
If an order is set to automatically invoice, this field is ignored. If all items have been invoiced earlier, then no invoice will be generated. 
Default is false.

SendInvoiceNotification.description:
If set to true, this will send out an email notification to the invoice recipients set up for the customer, if an invoice did get generated as a result of marking the order as complete. 
Default is false.

MarkRelatedOpenChangeOrdersComplete.description: This is only applicable for parent orders where open change orders exist. If set to true, this will mark all the change orders which are in In Progress or Approved status as complete.

Notify.description:
If set to true, this will send out an email notification to the order contact if it is set up for the order. 
Default is false.

CancelRelatedOpenChangeOrders.description: This is only applicable for parent orders where open change orders exist. If set to true, this will cancel all the change orders which are in In Progress or Approved status.

NewQty and QtyAdjustment values will be ignored at that time.

New overall quantity for the item. If UOM is used, specify the quantity in the base UOM. 
Either NewQty or QtyAdjustment should be specified. If both are specified. NewQty will take precedence. 
If there are more than one inventory locations do not use this field, Use the LocationLineItems. 
This will be ignored if the adjustment is just a value adjustment.

The quantity that needs to be adjusted for the item. If UOM is used, specify the quantity in the base UOM. 
Either NewQty or QtyAdjustment should be specified. If both are specified NewQty will take precedence. 
If there are more than one inventory locations do not use this field, Use the LocationLineItems. 
This will be ignored if the adjustment is just a value adjustment.

New overall quantity for the item at the location specified. 
If UOM is used, specify the quantity in the base UOM. Either LocationNewQty or LocationQtyAdjustment should be specified. 
If both are specified, LocationNewQty will take precedence.This will be ignored if the adjustment is just a value adjustment.

LocationQtyAdjustment.description:
The quantity that needs to be adjusted for the item at the location specified. 
If UOM is used, specify the quantity in the base UOM. Either LocationNewQty or LocationQtyAdjustment should be specified. 
If both are specified, LocationNewQty will take precedence. This will be ignored if the adjustment is just a value adjustment.

Id.description: Read-only. Item Build unique id - auto generated by the system.

Item.description: The item that is being built. Can only be a manufactured item

InventoryLocation.description: The item inventory location. Required when there are more than one locations.

Class.description: Build Class - required if Class tracking is on.

TxnDate.description: Build date - required. Today's date will be used in case is not specified.

TxnNumber.description: Build reference number - auto generated by the system for new builds if not provided. Required on update.

AllowDuplicateTxnNumber.description: Set to true if you want to skip duplicate check for TxnNumber

QtyToBuild.description: Quantity that needs to be built.

Status.description: The status of the build.The status of new builds can explicitly be set to any of the following: Incomplete (182), Pending (186), Finalized (170). There will be a validation check if the status is set as Pending OR Finalized to make sure the quantity of BOM is available. This property is read-only for existing builds.

AttachmentCount.description: Read-only. The number of attachments uploaded to the build.

DateCreated.description: Read-only. The date and time (in clients time zone) when the build was created.

CreatedBy.description: The person who created the build.

LastUpdatedDate.description: Read-only. The date and time (in clients time zone) when the build was last updated.

LastUpdatedBy.description: Read-only. The person who last updated the build.

Reviewed.description: Read-only. Indicates whether the build has been reviewed or not.

DateReviewed.description: Read-only. The date and time (in clients time zone) when the build was reviewed.

ReviewedBy.description: Read-only. The person who reviewed the build.

BoMComponentId.description: The unique id of the component in the Bill of Materials - this can be obtained by querying the “GET v1/items/{id}” endpoint for a manufactured item, and checking the Id in the GroupItem collection

Item.description: The item for this line item.

InventoryLocation.description: The item inventory location. Required for Inventory and Manufactured item types when there are more than one locations.

DateCreated.description: The date and time (in company time zone) when the item build was created.

LastUpdatedDate.description: The date and time (in company time zone) when the item build was last updated.

LastUpdatedDateRange.description: The date and time (in company time zone) when the build was last updated.

SortExpression.description: Available Sort Expresstions: [BuildDate Default DESC, Id, RefNumber, Item, Status, DateCreated, LastUpdatedDate]

Manufacturer.description: Manufacturer. Available for Inventory, Non Inventory, and Manufactured item types only.

ManufacturePartNumber.description: Manufacturer Part Number. Available for Inventory, Non Inventory, and Manufactured item types only.

Description.description: Description. Used on transaction line items when item is selected.

Price.description: Item Price. Available for Inventory, Non Inventory, Service, and Manufactured item types only.

Cost.description: Item Cost. Available for Inventory, Non Inventory, Service, and Manufactured item types only.

Taxable.description: Taxable. Available for Inventory, Non Inventory, Service, Item Group, and Manufactured item types only.

Weight.description: Weight. Available for Inventory, Non Inventory, Service, and Fixed Asset item types only.

ShippingPrice.description: Shipping Price. Available for Inventory, Non Inventory, Service, and Fixed Asset item types only.

ShippingPerc.description: Shipping Perc. Available for Inventory, Non Inventory, Service, and Fixed Asset item types only.

IncomeGLAccount.description: Income GL Account. Available for Inventory, Non Inventory, Service, Discount, Fixed Asset and Manufactured item types only. The default GL Account will be used if not provided.

InventoryAssetGLAccount.description: Inventory Asset GL Account. Available for Inventory and Manufactured item types only. The default Asset Account will be used if not provided.

COGSGLAccount.description: COGS GL Account. Available for Inventory and Manufactured item types only. The default COGS Account will be used if not provided.

ExpenseGLAccount.description: Expense GL Account. Available for Non Inventory, Service item types only. The default Expense Account will be used if not provided.

DefaultGLCategory.description: Default GL Category. Available for Inventory, Non Inventory, Service, Discount, Fixed Asset and Manufactured item types only.

DefaultInventoryLocation.description: Default Inventory Location. Available for Inventory and Manufactured item types only.

RelatedAsset.description: Related Asset. Available for Fixed Asset item type only.

AllowVendorToIncludeInBill.description: Allow Vendor To Include In Bill.

ReorderPoint.description: Reorder Point. Available for Inventory and Manufactured item types only.

ReorderAmount.description: Reorder Amount. Available for Inventory and Manufactured item types only.

QtyOnHand.description: Read-only. Available for Inventory and Manufactured item types only. The quantity is calculated as of today and is not location specific.

QtyAvailable.description: Read-only. Available for Inventory and Manufactured item types only. The quantity is calculated as of today and is not location specific.

BaseUnitOfMeasure.description:
Read-only. Base Unit of Measure. Available for Inventory, Non-Inventory, Service, Fixed Asset, Manufactured, and Deposit item types and only applicable if Unit of Measure tracking is enabled
Id: Base Unit of Measure Id of the Unit of Measure set configured at the item level
Name: Base Unit of Measure Abbreviation

GroupItems.description: Component items of the group. Available for Item Group and Manufactured item types only.

otherwise, it is ignored

Id.description: Read-only. Payment unique id - auto generated by the system.

TxnNumber.description: Read-only. Payment number - auto generated by the system.

Customer.description: The customer the payment was received from.

PaymentMethod.description: Payment method used for the payment. These are non-processing payment methods. Either PaymentMethod or PaymentProcessingMethod is required.

PaymentProcessingMethod.description: Payment processing method used for the payment. Actual payment processing will be done. PaymentProfile is required. Either PaymentMethod or PaymentProcessingMethod is required.

PaymentProfile.description: Payment profile used for the payment. Required when PaymentProcessingMethod is selected.

TxnDate.description: Date the payment is being made.

ReceivedFrom.description: The contact person the payment was received from.

ReferenceNumber.description: Reference number for the payment received.

Notes.description: Notes to provide additional information for the payment.

UnappliedAmount.description: Read-only. The amount of the payment that has not been applied yet.

BankGLAccount.description: This value is only required if the payment directly needs to be deposited into a bank account as per accounting settings.

ARGLAccount.description: Accounts Receivable associated with the payment. Required when more than one AR account is available.

IsDeposited.description: Read-only. Indicates whether the payment has been deposited or not. Deposited payments cannot be voided unless they are undeposited.

AutoApply.description: Indicates whether the payment should be auto applied to open invoices. Will always apply to the oldest open invoice first.

AppliedToInvoices.description: The invoices the payment was applied to. This property is also designated to be used while creating a payment which will apply the payment to the specified invoices.

AttachmentCount.description: Read-only. The number of attachments uploaded to the payment.

DateCreated.description: Read-only. The date and time (in company time zone) when the payment was created.

ReviewedBy.description: Read-only. The person who reviewed the payment.
